You are a deterministic JSON segmentation engine for scraped fiction.
Convert the cleaned input text into strict JSON for a multi-speaker TTS pipeline.
Return a JSON object with exactly these top-level keys:
{"characters": ["Name1"], "segments": [{"text": "<exact text>", "type": "dialogue"|"thought"|"general", "speaker": "<character>"|"narrator"|null}]}
Rules:
1. Preserve reading order and include all story text.
2. Use type=dialogue for spoken text, thought for internal monologue, general for everything else.
3. For narration/general text, speaker should be narrator or null.
4. Return strict JSON only with no markdown or commentary.

{"text": "← Back to novel
The Adventurer, 25 Years Reincarnated, Became an Academy Instructor
Chapter 487
RAWS

Chapter 486: Roman -3-
Thump. Thump. Thump.
"Mom! Dad! I'm home!"
When Alice knocked on the wooden door and called out, a commotion arose inside the house.
From beyond the door, the voices and actions felt filled with anticipation and joy.
With a 'Clack!' sound, her mother, Jane, flung the door wide open.
Roman could tell whose genes Alice's skin and beauty strongly inherited.
"Alice!!! Welcome ho-me?"
Jane habitually lowered her head to look at Alice. Confirming someone else was there, she slowly raised her head and looked at Roman.
"Hello. My name is Roman."
"Oh, hello...?"
As Roman, holding a gift, bowed his head, his eye level roughly matched Jane's. Jane reflexively responded to the greeting, her long ears twitching, clearly flustered. Alice spoke with the brightest voice possible, along with Tero.
"Mom! This is the Platinum Adventurer, Roman-nim!"
Sssshhh!
Regardless of Tero scrambling onto Roman's head and perking up, Jane, having grasped the situation from her daughter's words, gaped and froze. Dorian, who had heard Alice from inside, came running out with a 'Clatter-bang!'
..
..
For the gift to bring during the visit, I tried hard to choose something not too expensive or burdensome with Alice. Their choice was a set of mug cups sold at a pottery workshop located in the capital. A typical household might find the price too high for everyday cups, but as a gift, it was an item with a price that could somehow be understood.
Seeing the mother and daughter organizing the cups in the kitchen, Roman shifted his gaze back. Roman, with his large frame, bowed his head while seated towards Dorian, who was fidgeting nervously.
"I apologize for dropping by so late. I thought Alice had mentioned it when she sent the letter."
Roman hadn't known that she hadn't said a word about bringing him along in the letter. Whether it was a surprise of sorts, if the goal was to surprise her parents, it was a perfectly successful one.
"Not at all...! It was actually us, my husband and I, who should have visited and paid our respects! We feel sorry that you had to come all the way to Edelman despite being the ones who should be expressing gratitude."
Dorian across from him shifted in his seat, muttering continuously in an awkward posture, unable to properly sit or stand.
"Please, feel free to speak comfortably."
"How could I possibly do that? I, Dorian, hope Roman-nim will treat me comfortably."
Dorian was acting so deferentially because he was truly realizing the presence of a Platinum. In reality, it was a situation more significant than if any high-ranking noble had barged into their home. Frankly, if told to leave the house, he'd have to leave without a word. That Roman, possessing such power, was meticulously using polite speech and showing such formality made Dorian feel breathless.
"I came here with my face hardened like iron because there's something I need to tell you."
For a Platinum to come all the way to the hick town of Edelman with something to say... At Roman's words, Dorian's Adam's apple bobbed as he swallowed nervously. Based on observation, the atmosphere wasn't bad, but a lingering sense of 'what if' took precedence.
"Did Alice perhaps make a mistake with Roman-nim...?"
"No. Alice is doing wonderfully. Not only with her adventurer duties, but she's also excellently performing her role as an assistant instructor at Lofti Academy, which has given me some breathing room."
"...Thank you. That's truly a relief."
Though I had heard it through letters, hearing unbelievable stories directly from Roman's mouth made it feel incredibly real. An instructor at Lofti Academy, even as an assistant, was a position unimaginable for anyone in this backwater town of Edelman. It was truly like a dragon emerging from a ditch.
'Alice...'
She was already navigating the world better than Dorian himself. Realizing his daughter's growth brought a lump to his throat, making his nose tingle.
"Roman-nim! Have some fruit!"
It was a timely salvation for Dorian. Alice, holding a plate of neatly sliced peaches, returned to the table, pulling Jane along.
"I cut them nicely, didn't I? I can cut fruit just as well as Rike now."
"That's right. Everyone has good dexterity."
Jane was about to apologize from behind for not having prepared much, but sensing something from Alice's face and actions as she chattered while clinging tightly to Roman, she shut her mouth.
"Thank you for the treat."
"Here! This is the guest fork!"
Alice naturally took the seat next to Roman and offered him a peach slice with the fork. Jane, sitting next to Dorian, stiffened with her back straight, sensing a strange atmosphere.
A household filled only with Alice's chatter. At this point, Roman started to feel awkward too.
'Hmm, this atmosphere...'
He wondered if it was okay to bring up their daughter's story with the couple frozen like stone. Experiencing Dorian's distant attitude made Roman become more cautious as well.
Mhuuuuu.
Roman, chewing a peach while pondering, raised his gaze.
'A spirit?'
Its appearance was so small, one might mistake it for a tiny cloud or a cotton ball. The spirit Jane controlled was about the size of a fist, floating lazily through the air. Roman's affinity with spirits, gained from defeating the wanderer Deganello, drew the spirit. It floated gently and settled on Roman's lap. If Tero, who was already occupying Roman's head, hadn't been there, it surely would have landed on his head.
"It's a spirit as cute as Tero."
Sssit!
"Y-You can really see spirits, Adventurer-nim...?"
This was something even Jane, who had been watching nervously, couldn't help but break her silence to ask. Of course, it's not impossible for others to see them, so she accepted that. But the fact that Tero, whom Alice controlled, and her own contracted spirit were approaching and following someone else was strange.
"Yes. However, I heard it's not good to touch another person's spirit. So when they approach, I just offer my body like this."
"Mom! I told you. It's real!"
Mhuuu!
Seeing the spirit lounging on Roman's thigh let out a pleased cry, Jane's eyes widened.
"A spirit approaching first, how is that possible...?"
Tero never approached Jane so affectionately, and even Anila, Jane's own spirit, despite spending a long time with Alice, had never shown such familiarity towards Roman. That was common sense regarding spirits. For Jane, whose profession was research, this situation sparked extreme curiosity.
"Jane."
"Umm... I apologize."
Dorian placed a hand on Jane's shoulder as her emotions began to heighten over her area of interest. Dorian was the only one present who couldn't see the spirit. As Jane became increasingly excited, Dorian, conversely, felt himself calming down and cautiously opened the conversation.
"We knew Alice was coming. I heard from a student in the afternoon class that there was a disturbance in the bustling district. They said magic was even used, so I thought I had to go check immediately."
"That was Olla and Bona first...!"
Seeing Alice raise her voice in reaction, Dorian raised his hand to calm his daughter.
"I don't intend to punish you for this. If Alice did it, there must have been a reason."
"Our daughter has a kind heart."
Dorian nodded at Jane's words and continued speaking to Roman.
"I thought if we went straight to the bustling district, we might miss each other. If it were Olla and Bona's parents, they'd likely storm our guild, so I decided to wait."
Alice pouted but looked anxiously at Dorian, wondering if something had happened. Roman also chimed in, with a hint of concern.
"If there were any damages, I will take responsibility one way or another. It was I who pushed Alice to handle the aftermath."
"Nothing happened. Since you didn't come, I just finished work and went home on time."
Alice breathed a sigh of relief upon hearing that nothing had happened.
"Besides, it wasn't just Olla and Bona there, but also Edelman's Roman... should I call him that? Anyway, that guy was there too. Since he knew who Roman-nim was, he must have told them to keep quiet."
"Hmm, that guy..."
Since there was someone with the same name in front of him, he was cautious about speaking ill of him carelessly.
'Even that damned guy has some sense when his life is on the line.'
Dorian had been prepared since hearing that Alice had taken action against Olla and Bona. Weren't their parents known as particularly overbearing figures even in Edelman? Yet, the day had been strangely quiet. The question surrounding that was now neatly resolved.
In fact, Dorian hadn't only heard that news today. He had also heard rumors from a disciple interested in outside affairs that were causing a stir in the capital. That the Empire's 5 Knights and the Church's Saint were in 'that kind of relationship' with the Platinum Adventurer. The name of the adventurer at the center of the topic was all too familiar to Dorian.
'The fact that he has time to come all the way here... is it just a baseless rumor?'
Dorian dared not ask. He wasn't even sure if he was in a position to exchange pleasantries with a Platinum, even if treated kindly. It could be a false rumor that would offend Roman right before him, or perhaps something true that he wouldn't want to mention.
Roman, on the opposite side, quietly watched the couple. Under the table, he could feel Alice holding his hand, playfully fiddling with her fingers, acting affectionately.
'I have to take the plunge.'
As Dorian and Jane remained silent with complicated expressions, Roman made a decision and decided to get to the point. If things continued like this, even if they waited until nightfall, he doubted they'd get a chance to speak naturally, let alone have dinner.
"As I mentioned earlier, the reason I came all this way to see you both is because there is something I need to say."
"We are listening intently..."
When Roman spoke without warning, the startled couple leaned forward, focusing intently. Taking a breath, Roman downed the cold water on the table in one gulp, then looked squarely at the couple and spoke, pouring as much emotion as possible into his voice to make it more persuasive.
"Alice and I are dating seriously with the intention of marriage. I apologize for not informing you of our relationship beforehand."
"...What?"
His mind couldn't process the information directly. Dorian and Jane blinked. Alice, with a huge smile on her face, cupped her flushed cheeks with both hands, squirming shyly.
"I have come here to ask for your permission to marry. Please grant us your blessing for Alice's marriage."
The atmosphere, which had started to loosen up, froze once again.

Next →

Chapter 487: Roman -4-
"I've come to receive your permission for marriage. Please grant me your blessing to marry Alice."
Unlike the couple who froze like stone at Roman's words, Alice reacted the most intensely, her face turning bright red.
'Kyaaa~!!'
Alice shrieked with joy, stomping her feet and exclaiming about how unbearable this ticklish feeling was.
Since the words were out, there was no need to hide anything anymore. The girl clung tightly to her lover, resting her shoulder against his and leaning her head on him.
Shaaaa!
Mwoohuuu.
The spirits indebted to Roman's body seemed to resonate, letting out sounds. To the ears of the girl in love, those cries sounded no different from a serenade.
"Heeheehee!"
Alice let out a playful, nasal giggle as she imagined a happy future with Roman, but then she sensed something amiss in the lingering silence and snapped her eyes open, looking ahead.
"Mom, Dad... why aren't you saying anything?"
"Alice. Your father and mother just need some time."
Even with Roman's gentle reassurance, Alice didn't relax the tension in her eyes. Nor did her talking stop. Roman, holding the prestigious Platinum rank, had come all the way to Edelman to formally ask for their permission. The situation where a clear answer wasn't immediately forthcoming was something Alice couldn't even imagine.
"Do you want me to live alone and be a lonely spinster?"
"...There's no way we'd want that."
Dorian finally spoke, which only made Alice's emotions surge further.
"Then do you want me to marry some coward who abandoned me in a dungeon, or some fool like Ullah's younger brother, Stranden?"
Jane and Dorian immediately denied it, shaking their heads. How could they possibly want that? For the couple, their daughter's happiness was always the top priority.
"Alice, that's enough."
Roman understood the reason for the silence from the two people opposite him. He stopped Alice, whose voice was about to rise in fury.
"R-Roman-nim... Still, this is really not okay!!"
Alice was agitated by the unexpected anxiety, wondering if she should oppose them if her parents didn't agree.
"It's alright. I'll explain slowly."
Her expression softened slightly when he firmly clasped her hand under the table. Roman, who had handed Alice a peach, spoke in a low voice within the home.
"I may not have the power to see the future or read minds, but I dare to answer that I know what you are worried about."
"I'll start with the honest truth. Though I am undeserving, I have a total of seven lovers, including Alice. Among them are figures renowned throughout the Empire."
This was the part that bothered Roman the most, given his values from his past life, but Dorian and Jane merely showed mild surprise.
"Even so, I promise that Alice will never be unjustly oppressed or discriminated against in her treatment simply because she is not a noble."
'I've hit the mark,' Roman thought, sensing their genuine surprise this time. Watching how this society functioned, he felt he had built up common sense, so he understood that for Dorian and Jane, this was difficult to accept.
'How much should I mention?'
He couldn't exactly prove that his mansion wasn't a place where women fought fiercely, creating blizzards of tension, or where schemes were hatched to seize the family succession. He had to convey, through his words, that Alice's current living situation was different from that of the nobles or wealthy individuals commonly known.
"It's completely different from the picture you're worried about, Mom and Dad. I'm not just looking to live a life as a broodmare or a living collection."
Appropriate supporting words. The direct terms Roman hesitated to use were spoken instead by Alice.
"Alice and the Saintess of the Order, Atra, are friends of the same age, and their relationship is by no means based on one-sided favors. As for the Dritna sisters-"
"Even if I marry Roman-nim, my training won't stop, and I won't become complacent. Just as Roman-nim said, while fulfilling my duties as a spouse, I will also pursue what I want-"
As the stories unfolded, the couple listened intently. And then, they were trapped in a cascade of past memories, recalling them. Had Alice ever shown interest in the opposite sex during her life? No, did she even have the leisure for such things in her life? The child who strove to overcome the low birthright bestowed by her flawed parents, running relentlessly towards the ceiling without a single complaint, always seemed precarious even as she grew. He worried she might break or snap if she only looked upwards, and he was even afraid that she might fall apart if she lost her driving force and stopped moving.
But.
'Has this time already come?'
Seeing his daughter introduce the partner of her life, he felt a strange emotion. He was keenly realizing that the day he had only imagined as a parent had arrived. Alice, who used to sleep all day in her cradle and toddle while holding onto her parents' legs, had grown up so much. Listening to the story, Alice's parents heard answers to their concerns, and they felt firsthand that Alice was living happily in a much better environment than they had imagined. Even if there were aspects they didn't fully understand, what else could parents do if not trust their child? They simply accepted it blindly and nodded.
"That is all I wished to convey."
"...Thank you."
When Roman finished his explanation, Dorian and Jane bowed their heads. Then, Jane turned to Alice and spoke.
"Alice..."
"Yes."
Their worries melted away, and the parents' true feelings began to surface. Just as Dorian supported Jane, and Jane supported Dorian, like two people who weren't blood-related but sustained each other. They felt relief, and guilt, that Alice, who had only run forward, had found someone to be her pillar and someone she could lean on.
"Mom was also new to being a mom... I'm sorry."
She should have smiled and congratulated her the moment she heard it. She realized her mistake and regretted it when she remained frozen, unable to speak. How anxious and sad must Alice have felt when the parents she trusted didn't respond? While feeling relieved that she had gained enoughä½è£ to look elsewhere, the worries that entered her mind made it difficult to speak easily.
"Sob..."
As Jane's eyes moistened and her voice trembled with tears, Dorian wiped his spouse's eyes. Alice buried her face in Roman's arm, hiding her expression. Dorian, who had comforted Jane, who was overcome with emotion and sniffling, looked up at Roman.
"...May I ask one thing?"
"Of course. I will answer with all sincerity."
Husband and wife are one body. While some couples might not be like that, Dorian and Jane were certain they were thinking the same thing right now.
"Roman-nim, why do you seek our couple's consent?"
After all, the other party was Roman, the pinnacle of adventurers, a Platinum. Even if he had impregnated Alice, who was so smitten, or informed them after the marriage was done, Dorian and Jane would have had no room to argue. So why? Why? For what reason? A simple couple with no titles and only their beautiful daughter as their pride, why did he bow his head and ask for their approval?
"It truly is a difficult world to make a living, isn't it?"
Roman comforted Alice, who was smearing tears and snot all over his clothes, and continued his next words in a gentle tone.
"If you ask anyone what a parent's duty is, they'll likely give a plausible answer about being responsible for and raising their children, but parents who truly overcome overwhelming hardships and fulfill that duty are rare in this world."
"Though it was the last time when I was young, I remember my parents' faces. My parents, on the verge of starvation, lost their reason and tried to trade me for the neighbor's child."
Dorian wasn't someone who wouldn't understand what those words implied. But perhaps Roman was using such parents as a negative example, or perhaps there was something hidden within his answer to his question. Dorian couldn't quite grasp Roman's true intentions.
"Do you resent them...?"
"I do not resent them."
The answer came out so clearly, as if there was no need to think about it. Dorian was speechless, but thinking that he wouldn't hear it if not now, he forced himself to speak.
"May I ask why?"
"I do not resent them because I saw my parents weeping then. Knowing how terrifying the fear of approaching starvation is, I have no intention of blaming their choice."
It wasn't that Roman's parents saw the anticipation of future fullness in their eyes at that moment. At that time, Roman's parents were crushed by guilt and sin, and their eyes were filled only with fear. Having laid the foundation, it was time to answer Dorian's question. Roman, uncharacteristically, avoided the gaze opposite him slightly, feeling the words were somewhat embarrassing.
"I know that you must have endured much hardship and toil after being given the title of parents."
"I wanted to express my gratitude for enduring those difficult days and raising Alice so uprightly."
Roman wanted Alice to maintain a harmonious relationship with her parents even after marriage. If he were to force things through power or authority, there was a possibility that relationship could falter, so he had to be cautious.
"I wish for you both to be present at the wedding ceremony. Please grant me permission for my marriage to Alice."
When Roman bowed his head respectfully once more after finishing his explanation, Dorian and Jane pushed their chairs back and stood up, bowing deeply to Roman.
"Please take good care of our daughter."

Next →

Chapter 488: Roman -5-
As soon as permission was granted and the atmosphere lightened in a positive direction, the first ones to react were the spirits.
Tero, who had been itching to move while watching their reactions, went wild.
Shaat!
Moohoo!
Under the dining table, Tero and Anila. A lizard the size of a fist and a lamb tumbled around, tangled together.
I don't know what the spirits were doing, bickering amongst themselves, but they must surely be on good terms.
"When do you plan to return to the capital?"
"Alice wanted to have a reunion with you both... so we plan to depart leisurely tomorrow."
Dorian and Jane's gazes shifted towards their daughter.
They gazed at Alice, who had been hiding her face and crying softly until now.
As parents, they felt a sense of satisfaction, yet were swept up in an indescribable emotion. Alice probably couldn't clearly explain her own feelings either.
"And this is something I'd like you both to consider."
After receiving permission, they strove to understand each other.
They made an effort to share as many stories as possible.
There, Roman suggested to Alice's parents that they live in the capital.
It wasn't a sudden proposal out of the blue. He was only bringing it up after hearing honest opinions from everyone at the mansion.
There are other couples at the mansion, and while I understand the potential discomfort for everyone involved, finding a nice house in the capital is no trouble at all.
Dorian and Jane listened intently to Roman's proposal with surprised eyes.
When Roman finished speaking and quietly awaited their answer.
The couple exchanged a brief glance and Dorian spoke on their behalf.
"Thanks to our admirable daughter, we're hearing a proposal beyond our wildest dreams. Thank you so much. It feels like I'm dreaming just hearing it."
Just as Alice had anticipated, their thanks were followed by an extremely polite refusal.
There was absolutely no hint of hesitation or regret in Dorian's voice.
'They're good people.'
Even though Roman had said it as casually as possible, the burden of receiving accommodation in the capital for free must have been significant, but these two fundamentally possess a strong sense of moral responsibility.
Surely, for them, being able to see Alice, who is practically their everything, whenever they wished would be the greatest thing.
However, Dorian has disciples he is training, and Jane has research she is personally conducting.
Furthermore, they knew from past experience that constantly hovering near their daughter could unintentionally cause her concern.
Various reasons prevented them from accepting Roman's gracious offer.
In conclusion, Edelmann isn't so precious or wonderful that this couple couldn't leave it. However, they intend to fulfill the duties assigned to them.
For Dorian and Jane, Edelmann is a place where they lead a faithful daily life while waiting for their daughter to visit.
Roman could only admire them inwardly. It's truly difficult to meet people like this, whether in a past life or now.
Hearing this, I don't think it's foolish to refuse an opportunity for a life of luxury.
"When the time is right, I will propose it to you both again."
"We will consider it carefully as we go about our lives."
When Roman didn't push stubbornly and backed off easily.
Dorian showed a relaxed smile for the first time.
After that, Alice's parents did their best to get closer to Roman, just as much as he tried.
Jane prepared the late dinner she had been postponing, and Dorian engaged in light, feather-like conversation with Roman while waiting for their daughter to calm down.
Alice stopped crying and revealed her swollen eyes right when the meal was ready.
From Jane's perspective, it was fortunate that she had stocked up on ingredients after receiving Alice's letter.
"I wonder if it will suit your taste."
"It's deadly delicious. My palate is quite similar to Alice's."
The thoughtfulness of parents waiting for their daughter. At a glance, the table was filled only with dishes Alice loved.
"...Mom. Dad."
"What is it?"
Alice, who had been sniffling while putting food into her mouth, finally spoke.
The girl's voice, subtly trembling, still held remnants of her emotions.
"Can we all come visit together next time? I want to show you what kind of wonderful people I'm living with."
Alice's voice, saying she would bring the Saint of the Order and the Empire's 5 Knights.
Dorian and Jane, despite imagining a scenario so overwhelming that it choked them and made food impossible to swallow.
reflexively nodded at their daughter's teary eyes, signaling their consent.
··
··
"I was thinking of getting a room for you in the bustling part of Edelmann and sending you there."
Finishing the meal, Roman stood up, looking at the pitch-darkness outside the window. He said this intending for the family to spend their long-awaited time together.
Even though this had been discussed beforehand, Alice pouted, displeased, and expressed her dissatisfaction. However, she couldn't object because she had previously agreed, considering her parents' position.
"How could we possibly send our guest and future son-in-law out at this late hour?"
Since Alice didn't stop him, Dorian caught Roman.
Roman and Alice's lips twitched at the word 'son-in-law'.
"I'm really fine."
"I know you're saying that out of consideration for us. However, if you, Roman-nim, who went to the trouble of visiting us, were to stay at an inn, it would put us in a more difficult position."
"Besides, rumors spread incredibly fast in Edelmann. If word gets out that we made the son-in-law from the capital sleep outside, we wouldn't be able to show our faces in public."
They mentioned having spare blankets and rooms, seeming ready to offer their own bed to Roman. Roman, finding that prospect too awkward to bear, politely declined, and the girl intervened.
"Roman-nim should naturally sleep in my room with me!"
Alice's bright yellow eyes flashed, seizing the opportunity. Before Dorian could say anything, Alice grabbed Roman's sleeve and pulled him along.
Roman followed his lover, who was leading him to her room.
Click! The door connecting to the living room closed.
"Hooo..."
"So this is the room Alice used to stay in."
Alice's room had a small bed. It was barely large enough for Roman's feet to stick out if he lay down. Although the room had been unused for quite some time, Jane managed it daily so Alice could stay comfortably whenever she returned, ensuring no dust accumulated. By the window, there was a pot filled with moist soil, perhaps for Tero.
"I-it's kind of embarrassing..."
Alice's expression softened, drenched in affection. Looking back, the girl placed her hand over her chest, her heart pounding uncontrollably. Alice, her eyes still swollen from silent, great sobbing, kept realizing the situation as she looked at her lover.
"Are you starting to feel it now?"
Held in Roman's arms, Alice shyly nodded. The giddy feeling of being engaged, having received permission from her parents. She could hardly stay still.
'My husband is so cool...'
Shyness kept the word 'husband' from escaping her lips; she only called him that in her heart. Her own parents seemed genuinely moved in an instant. Alice herself had heard it clearly with her own ears - his eloquent words, filled with earnest sincerity, were so precious that they couldn't possibly refuse.
"R-Roman-nim..."
"Yes?"
"I've fallen for you all over again, Roman-nim..."
"It's my honor."
His words from when they arrived were echoed back. As Roman smiled and affectionately squeezed Alice's cheeks.
Alice stood on her tiptoes, squeezed her eyes shut, and pouted her lips like a duck.
"K-kiss... please!"
She couldn't handle any more intense physical intimacy right now. She definitely couldn't bear it.
Smack! The sound echoed as a satisfying sensation lingered on her lips. Alice cupped her burning cheeks with both hands.
'...My heart feels like it's going to explode.'
The girl's heart pounded violently, threatening to burst, and her face burned with fever. If Jane hadn't knocked on the door, announcing she'd brought extra blankets, who knows what might have happened driven by instinct afterward.
Although it was a room for just the two of them, the bed was too small to share. But Alice wasn't about to give up; Roman laid the blanket he received from Jane on the floor, and Alice, who had been on the bed, quietly got down and snuggled close. No matter what, he had no intention of making love in this cramped, humble home. If he used mana to block the sounds, it would be like advertising to Jane, a mage, that he was with her daughter. That wouldn't do. He had to maintain the image he'd built up, didn't he?
Despite that, the night was incredibly enjoyable rather than disappointing. Alice, snuggled under the blanket, discussed future plans and anticipated joys. As they exchanged stories of their past, like their first meeting at the Adventurer's Guild or their chance encounter in a dungeon, sunlight began to stream through the window. As the sun rose, the movements of the couple preparing for work could be felt beyond the door.
Roman and Alice decided to return to the capital that afternoon. It was after they had lunch together at Dorian and Jane's martial arts guild, who had made time for them.
"Well then, we shall take our leave. We will visit again soon with good news."
"We will be waiting. Alice, stay healthy and write to us."
"Okay! Mom and Dad, you stay healthy too!"
Before leaving, Roman stopped by the Edelmann Adventurer's Guild and made sure to greet the person in charge, leaving his mark. Having done some basic preventative work, he finally felt at ease and could depart.
'I guess I passed the test as a son-in-law.'
The visit to Edelmann, which was more eventful than expected, concluded with satisfactory results. The two boarded the gate with light hearts.
And so.
As they neared the mansion in the capital, with Alice chattering away, filling his ears with pleasantries. Roman sensed Gleipnir and Nachal, who seemed to have woken up fully rested from their slumber, making their presence known. At the same time, he began to worry about how he would explain these two to everyone.
